story.problem: Python's built-in urllib is painfully verbose — you need dozens of lines just to make a POST request with headers and parse the response. Before requests, every team wrote their own wrapper or copied boilerplate. Kenneth Reitz published requests in 2011 and the community never looked back.
story.mental_model: Think of requests as a polished envelope system for HTTP: you stuff your data in, address it with a URL and headers, send it, and get a clean Response object back. The library handles encoding, redirects, cookies, and keep-alive connections so you don't have to.
story.when_to_use: Use requests for any synchronous HTTP work: calling REST APIs, scraping web pages, downloading files, or integrating with webhooks. It's the right default for scripts, CLI tools, and Django/Flask backends that don't need async.
story.when_not_to_use: Avoid requests when you're writing async code — it will block your event loop. Switch to httpx (drop-in async API) or aiohttp for FastAPI backends, async scrapers, or anything running inside asyncio.

alternatives.name: httpx
alternatives.reason: Drop-in async-compatible replacement; supports HTTP/2 and is the natural upgrade path

alternatives.name: aiohttp
alternatives.reason: Mature async HTTP client/server built specifically for asyncio workloads

alternatives.name: urllib3
alternatives.reason: Lower-level library that requests itself uses; reach for it only when you need fine-grained connection control

nodes.label: requests.get()
nodes.summary: Send a GET request. Returns a Response object.

nodes.label: requests.post()
nodes.summary: Send a POST request with optional JSON/form body.

nodes.label: requests.put()
nodes.summary: Send a PUT request to update a resource.

nodes.label: requests.patch()
nodes.summary: Send a PATCH request for partial updates.

nodes.label: requests.head()
nodes.summary: Send a HEAD request (headers only, no body).

nodes.label: Session
nodes.summary: Persistent session that shares headers, cookies, and connection pool across requests.

nodes.label: Request
nodes.summary: Represents a prepared HTTP request before sending.

nodes.label: PreparedRequest
nodes.summary: A fully prepared request ready to be sent via Session.send().

nodes.label: requests.auth
nodes.summary: Auth helpers: HTTPBasicAuth, HTTPDigestAuth, AuthBase for custom auth.

nodes.label: requests.exceptions
nodes.summary: Exception hierarchy: RequestException, ConnectionError, Timeout, HTTPError, TooManyRedirects.

nodes.label: requests.adapters
nodes.summary: HTTPAdapter for configuring retries, pool size, and SSL.

steps.label: Import
steps.code:
>>> import requests
>>> r = requests.get('https://httpbin.org/basic-auth/user/pass', auth=('user', 'pass'))
>>> r.status_code
200
>>> r.headers['content-type']
'application/json; charset=utf8'
>>> r.encoding
'utf-8'
>>> r.text
'{"authenticated": true, ...'
>>> r.json()
{'authenticated': True, ...}
